providers.name: Camp Arena Stage
providers.description: Multi-arts day camp run by DC's famous regional theatre: campers aged 8-15 mix theatre, music, creative writing, film and visual art in one program, ending in a festival of performances. One of the few serious arts camps where siblings across a wide age gap can attend together.
providers.locations: Georgetown Visitation Preparatory School, 1524 35th St NW
providers.price_detail: $1,400 per 2-week session; early drop-off and extended day extra
providers.financial_aid: Scholarships available — ask the camp office

reg_2027.notes: Multi-week sessions; popular sessions fill by spring — register at opening.

providers.status_2026: Jul 27-Aug 7 two-week session on the calendar; call to confirm seats
providers.fit_notes: Creative kids who want variety rather than one discipline; genuinely mixed-age

providers.name: Smithsonian Summer Camp
providers.description: 90+ themed one-week camps behind the scenes of the Smithsonian museums — history, art, science, design — for kids entering K through 9th grade, grouped by grade band. The flagship museum camp in the country, at the Ripley Center on the Mall.
providers.locations: S. Dillon Ripley Center, 1100 Jefferson Dr SW
providers.extended_care: Before-care 8-9am and after-camp to 6pm for a fee

reg_2027.mechanism: member lottery, then open sales mid-February
reg_2027.notes: Join Smithsonian Associates at Contributor level in December to enter the lottery; waitlists clear for 70%+ of campers.

providers.status_2026: Weeks remain through Aug 14 at all grade bands; popular camps waitlist-only

providers.name: Teen Naturalist Training
providers.description: Three-week field-naturalist training for teens entering grades 9-11 at the 40-acre Woodend Sanctuary in Chevy Chase: ecology fieldwork, species identification, conservation skills. The serious nature option for teens who have aged out of day camps.
providers.locations: Woodend Sanctuary, 8940 Jones Mill Rd, Chevy Chase MD

reg_2027.notes: Three sessions across the summer; apply early — small cohorts.

providers.fit_notes: Outdoorsy teens; not a drop-off day camp — expects real interest

providers.name: DC DPR Summer Camps
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation
providers.description: The District's own camps at rec centers citywide: Discovery day camps (6-10), Boost specialty camps for tweens (robotics, journalism, improv, CSI), aquatics, therapeutic rec, and teen career camps. The budget backbone of DC summers.
providers.price_detail: Ward-based rates; 50-75% reduced rates for income-qualified DC residents

reg_2027.mechanism: lottery (2 weeks), then rolling first-come until filled
reg_2027.notes: Non-lottery leftovers appear on DPRprograms.com through the summer.

providers.status_2026: Session 4 (Aug 3-14) waitlists + rolling leftovers on the portal
providers.fit_notes: DC residents get priority and reduced rates; nonresidents pay more if space allows
